mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month PAYEMS trend signals expanding labor income and stronger expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 141126 thousand for 2015-02-01
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth does not produce a positive 20-day return spread versus lower PAYEMS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate breakout above its prior low-rate range signals the start of monetary tightening, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuation multiples over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average by more than 0.25pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.11 percent for 2015-02-01
regime_hint: transition from zero-rate policy to monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate while unemployment declines, household spending momentum and labor income reinforce each other, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds 1.0% and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 437006 million dollars for 2015-02-01
fact:UNRATE vintage value was 5.5 percent for 2015-02-01
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and UNRATE-down trigger fails to beat unconditional 20-day returns by at least 25 bps on average in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening real activity and margin pressure in cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contraction episodes have median 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than zero or if downside hit rate is not above the unconditional negative-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation turns negative while the federal funds rate is stuck near zero, deflation risk raises real debt burdens and weakens nominal revenue expectations, causing negative 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses below 0% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.11 percent for 2015-02-01
failure_condition: Reject if zero-rate deflation triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts under low short rates signals expanding construction activity and durable-goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month growth crosses above 5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 1065 thousand units for 2015-01-01
regime_hint: housing-led expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST-up and low-FEDFUNDS trigger does not generate a positive average 60-day S&P 500 return or fails to outperform unconditional returns by at least 50 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth indicates broad demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 16293.7 billions of chained 2017 dollars for 2014-10-01
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth quarters do not have higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-positive growth quarters in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 5.5 percent for 2015-02-01
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below zero.
